data.label: VAD filter
data.description: Borrows the uploaded WAV from Start via `{{ start.audio }}` (compiler stages it via StoragePath, runner resolves to a local path). Reads it with the stdlib `wave` module, runs a simple energy-gate VAD over 30 ms frames (pure-Python `audioop` RMS — swap webrtcvad/silero for production), and re-emits ONLY the voiced frames as raw 16-bit PCM over the Data/Out channel `speech` (~100 ms per write). The bulk audio bytes flow out-of-band over the JetStream datastream transport — they NEVER enter the net marking; the net sees only the channel's open + close (docs/25).

data.label: Transcribe (faster-whisper)
data.description: Declares a Data/In channel `speech` wired from the VAD step's open handle. `aithericon.stream('speech')` drains the out-of-band voiced-audio byte stream (starting EARLY, while the VAD step is still producing), reassembles the raw 16-bit PCM, and runs faster-whisper (`tiny`, CPU/int8) over it. Instead of returning one transcript, it EMITS each recognized segment as it is produced onto the Control/Out channel `parts` (`aithericon.out('parts')` → open → item* → close): one bracketed episode, one tiny `{index,text,start,end}` token per segment. The producer does NOT decide the fold — the consumer edge (`join: gather`) collects them in the `collect` step. LIVE-ONLY: first run pip-installs faster-whisper (ctranslate2/numpy) and downloads the `tiny` model (~75 MB) into the venv/HF cache — slow + network-dependent, exceeds the 60 s `mekhan test` cap on a cold run.

data.label: Collect transcript
data.description: Consumes the transcribe step's Control channel `parts` with `join: gather`, so the compiler's gather barrier re-orders the emitted segments by index and parks the whole collection as `{ output: [<segment>, ...] }` — fed in as this step's input token. Reads the list off `input.output`, concatenates the segment texts in order, and emits the complete `transcript` (plus `segment_count` and total `seconds`). This is the consumer-side fold: the producer just emitted a stream, the join discipline lives on the edge.
